OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION, INTERROGATORIES AND FOR AN AWARD OF MONETARY SANCTIONS FILED BY DEFENDANT DITO, JOHN JOSEPH, IND. AND DBA JOHN J. DITO & SON

REPLY MEMO OF P&A ISO MTN FOR FURTHER DISCOVERY RESPONSES BY DEF JOHN JOSEPH DITO TO PLTFS' REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND THINGS [SET ONE]; FORM INTERROGATORIES, [SET ONE] AND FOR AWARD OF MONETARY SANCTIONS FILED BY PLAINTIFF OW, LILY OW, PEGGY

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFFS LILY OW AND PEGGY OW'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND THINGS (SET ONE), AND FORM INTERROGATORIES (SET ONE), FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS; AND FOR AWARD OF MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED. IT WAS THE OBLIGATION OF DEFENDANT, HAVING REQUESTED AN EXTENSION OF TIME, TO CONFIRM SAID EXTENSION IN WRITING. SEE CCP 2030.270(B) AND 2031.270(B). ONCE THE DISPUTE OVER WHICH EXTENSION DATE WAS OPERATIVE BECAME KNOWN AND THE PARTIES COULD NOT RESOLVE THE ISSUE THROUGH THE "MEET AND CONFER" PROCESS, THE STATUTORY SCHEME TO BE RELIEVED OF A WAIVER OF OBJECTIONS PURSUANT CCP 2030.290(A) AND 2031.300(A)BY THE FILING OF MOTION BECAME AVAILABLE. IT WAS NOT FOLLOWED. THE DEFENDANT, SIMULTANEOUSLY WITH HIS FILING OF ITS OPPOSITION HEREIN, ON FEBRUARY 27, 2014, SERVED FURTHER FORMAL RESPONSES. THE FACT OF THE LATE SERVICE DOES NOT MOOT CONSIDERATION OF THE ISSUES RAISED BY THIS MOTION. SEE YOUNGER ON CALIFORNIA MOTIONS (2011) PARA 29:56. BECAUSE THE COURT FINDS THE INITIAL SERVICE OF RESPONSES ON DECEMBER 19, 2013 WAS UNTIMELY, NO RULING ON THE ADEQUACY OF DEFENDANT' FURTHER INTERROGATORY RESPONSES AND PRODUCTION IS NECESSARY. BOTH DISCOVERY PLEADINGS SHALL BE RESPONDED TO, WITHOUT OBJECTION, WITHIN THIRTY DAYS FROM THE DATE OF SERVICE OF THIS ORDER. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED AGAINST DEFENSE COUNSEL ONLY THE SUM OF $2,500.00 TO BE PAID WITHIN THIRTY DAYS OF SERVICE OF THIS ORDER. THE AWARDING OF SANCTIONS TAKES INTO ACCOUNT THE FACT PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL OFFERED TO PROVIDE ADDITIONAL TIME FOR THE DEFENDANT TO RESPOND TO THE DISCOVERY AND THE OFFER WAS NOT ACCEPTED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE PRO-TEM: STEVEN STEIN, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jun-20-2014 Continued To Oct-27-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Ntc Of Fmtn For Order Compelling Further Responses To Req. For Production Of Documents And Things (Set One), And Form Interrogatories (Set One), For Production Of Documents; And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-12-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
PLAINTIFFS LILY OW and PEGGY OW'S Motion For Order Compelling Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents And Things (Set One), And Form Interrogatories (Set One), For Production Of Documents; And For Award Of Monetary Sanctions is granted. It was the obligation of defendant, having requested an extension of time, to confirm said extension in writing. See CCP 2030.270(b) and 2031.270(b). Once the dispute over which extension date was operative became known and the parties could not resolve the issue through the "meet and confer" process, the statutory scheme to be relieved of a waiver of objections pursuant CCP 2030.290(a) and 2031.300(a)by the filing of motion became available. It was not followed. The defendant, simultaneously with his filing of its Opposition herein, on February 27, 2014, served further formal responses. The fact of the late service does not moot consideration of the issues raised by this motion. See Younger on California Motions (2011) para 29:56. Because the Court finds the initial service of responses on December 19, 2013 was untimely, no ruling on the adequacy of defendant' further interrogatory responses and production is necessary. Both discovery pleadings shall be responded to, without objection, within thirty days from the date of service of this Order. Sanctions are awarded against defense counsel only the sum of $2,500.00 to be paid within thirty days of service of this Order. The awarding of sanctions takes into account the fact plaintiff's counsel offered to provide additional time for the defendant to respond to the discovery and the offer was not accepted. Order signed. Judge Pro-tem: Steven Stein, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not Reported. =(302/JPT)
